subject: An ornate ceremonial banner mounted on a tall pole, topped with a stylized serpent or naga skull motif and trailing a dark pennant; clan standard of the Sultai
description: A tall ceremonial standard rises from a swampy, overgrown ruin. The banner-pole is crowned with a sculpted serpentine head—jeweled or gem-set in the hollow eyes—suggesting the naga and reptilian iconography of the Sultai Brood. A long dark pennant or cloth hangs from beneath the finial, draped against a cluttered backdrop of carved stonework and decay. The palette leans muddy green, swamp blue, and bruised black with golden highlights on the metalwork. Atmosphere is humid, oppressive, and steeped in old, corrupt wealth.
foreground: ornate banner pole topped with serpent/naga skull finial and trailing pennant
background: swampy ruin with carved stonework and tangled vegetation
